findings.description: Photography style directive (warm, realistic lifestyle imagery depicting everyday activities) has no corresponding rule in the candidate set — this is a distinct imagery guidance rule that was dropped rather than clustered.

payload.description: Photography must use warm, realistic patient lifestyle imagery depicting everyday activities (walking the dog, beach, BBQ) to convey returning to normal life.

findings.description: The requirement that images be housed in the Accent shape (asymmetric rounded-corner crops, not plain rectangles/circles) is a distinct hard-constraint layout rule referencing §2.5 that is missing from the candidate rules entirely.

payload.description: Images must be cropped using the Accent shape (asymmetric rounded corners per §2.5); plain rectangles or circles are prohibited.

findings.description: Alt text rule is flagged as a 'brand accessibility rule' in source but the extracted rule carries no governance facet (e.g., accessibility standard reference, owner/enforcement body) despite explicit textual cue.

Missed relation: waves/gradients overlay rule (u_0003) applies specifically to photography already constrained by the Accent shape crop rule (u_0002); these two should be linked as related imagery-composition rules rather than left isolated.
